workflow.name: Compare LINE palm images and log Gemini health insights to Google Sheets
workflow.description:
## AI Palm Health Tracker – Overview

This workflow receives palm images sent via LINE and provides AI-generated health insights.

## Step-by-step process:
1.User sends a palm image via LINE
2.Webhook receives the image
3.Image is saved to Google Drive
4.Past records are checked in Google Sheets

If this is the first submission:
→ AI will perform a palm reading

If previous records exist:
→ Retrieve the latest saved image
→ Validate that two images are available
→ AI performs a comparison analysis

All results are saved in Google Sheets and sent back to the user via LINE.

workflow.name: AI Palm Health Tracker – LINE Image Comparison & Gemini Analysis Workflow

parameters.text:
=You are a health advisor with knowledge of both Western and Eastern medicine.

Treat the first item as today’s palm image and the second item as a previous palm image.
If the first image cannot be identified as a palm, output:
“Unable to detect a palm.”
Do not perform any of the following evaluations.

Then, compare the two palm images and provide health advice within 200 characters based on palm lines, dryness, and color tone.

If the second image does not exist or is not a palm image, do not perform any further evaluation.

Do not make a medical diagnosis. Write the text as general health awareness or observations about physical condition.

Output format:
【Today’s Health Check】Body text

No reply to me is required.

=You are an excellent palm reader.

The image sent is a palm image.
If it cannot be identified as a palm, output:
【Unable to identify the image as a palm】
Do not perform any of the following processing.

If the image is identified as a palm, provide the following output based on palm reading.

No reply to me is required.

Output content:
～For the first session, I will tell your fortune based on your palm～
【Life Line】 - (Score out of 10)
【Heart Line】 - (Score out of 10)
【Head Line】 - (Score out of 10)
【Fate Line】 - (Score out of 10)
【Overall Fortune】Provide overall advice based on the palm reading within 150 characters.

parameters.content:
## AI Palm Health Tracker – Overview

This workflow receives palm images sent via LINE and provides AI-generated health insights.

## Step-by-step process:
1.User sends a palm image via LINE
2.Webhook receives the image
3.Image is saved to Google Drive
4.Past records are checked in Google Sheets

If this is the first submission:
→ AI will perform a palm reading

If previous records exist:
→ Retrieve the latest saved image
→ Validate that two images are available
→ AI performs a comparison analysis

All results are saved in Google Sheets and sent back to the user via LINE.

## Setup Steps

1. Create a LINE Messaging API channel and obtain the Channel Access Token.
2. Create a Google Spreadsheet with the following columns: “days (date), pic (image ID), url (destination link), message (advice)”.
3. Create a Google Drive folder to store palm photos.
4. Add the Channel Access Token information to the Config node.
5. Set the Webhook URL in the LINE Developers Console.

## Key points:
• If a non-image message is sent, the user is prompted to send a palm image.
• If two images are not available, the comparison analysis is skipped.
• The advice provided does not constitute a medical diagnosis. It is intended solely as general health awareness guidance.

## LINE Webhook Trigger

This workflow is triggered via the LINE Messaging API webhook.
It receives palm images submitted by users and performs AI-based health analysis.

If the message type is not "image," the workflow responds with an instructional message.

**Security Note:**
Access tokens should be stored as environment variables within the config node.

## Image Storage Layer

Uploaded images are saved to Google Drive for historical comparison. Next, data stored in Google Sheets is retrieved.

The stored data includes:
* Timestamp
* Drive File ID
* Public viewing link
* AI response message

This makes it possible to track the condition of the palm over the long term. After that, the system checks whether any stored data exists.

## Historical Record Retrieval Logic

The workflow checks if previous palm records exist.

If no previous record:
→ Execute First-Time Palm Analysis

If previous record exists:
→ Retrieve the most recent image
→ Prepare for comparison analysis

This ensures adaptive AI behavior.

## AI Comparison Analysis

Google Gemini Vision model performs comparative analysis
between current and previous palm images.

The model:
- Detects palm presence
- Compares lines, dryness, and color tone
- Generates non-medical health insights (≤200 characters)

No medical diagnosis is provided.

## First-Time Palm Fortune Analysis

If this is the user's first submission,
the workflow performs a standalone palm reading.

Outputs:
- Life Line Score
- Heart Line Score
- Head Line Score
- Fate Line Score
- Overall Advice

This creates an engaging onboarding experience.

## Health Log Recording

All AI outputs are appended to Google Sheets.

This enables:

* Progress tracking
* Data auditing
* User history management
* Future analytics expansion

The message part of the AI output is also sent as a reply to the LINE user who submitted the image.

user.name: Hiroshi Hashimoto
user.bio:
I studied programming at a vocational school in Japan and obtained the Level 2 Information Technology Engineer certification. After that, I worked as a sales representative at a staffing agency.

In 2021, I opened a private tutoring school, where I serve as the head instructor, supporting elementary, junior high, and high school students with their studies. I also launched a QUREO programming school.

Since 2025, I have been studying n8n and am aiming to become a certified creator.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
